name: TAO 4h Delayed Retest Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a breakout is not chased immediately; price must retest the broken level, hold it, and only then continuation is entered
thesis: Cycle 77 includes breakout continuation with delayed entry after retest rather than immediate trigger. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the only allowed lane with trend-up contamination strong enough to plausibly reward a held retest. The edge is the accepted retest, not the first breakout bar.
invalidation_condition: If accepted retests still remain too sparse or repeatedly fail back into the old range, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks a local boundary, retests that level, holds it, and continues in the breakout direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed retests avoid the worst location of first-break chasing and exploit trapped countertrend inventory when the trend really resumes.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the only lane in the basket with enough live trend contamination to plausibly reward delayed-retest continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Retests can still be too infrequent or become late continuation entries that reverse quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but false retests dominate, tighten the hold tolerance before changing the boundary window.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than the ETH/SOL mean-reversion structures, but better asymmetry when continuation survives the retest.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and delayed-retest continuation shows cleaner asymmetry than recent sparse continuation families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated retests that immediately fail back through the breakout line.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because TAO trend contamination should create tradable retests

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit trend-capable lane for delayed-retest continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough accepted retests with positive post-cost asymmetry.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h retests remain too sparse or fail immediately back into range.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a few strong continuation runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same retest grammar survives on the cycle primary asset even if trend quality is lower.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 4h preserves some accepted retests with enough density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h confirms the idea needs stronger trend contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude and fewer retest continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same retest logic gains count on 1h without becoming noisy first-break chasing.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 1h increases count while preserving actual retest structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 1h degenerates into noisy intraday breakout churn.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller retest continuations than TAO 4h.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR follow-through
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR follow-through
